title: Career Agency, Success Criteria, and Search Constraints · Guided Evidence Sprint
summary: A guided career studio extends learner-owned career decision charter while retaining truthful evidence, accessibility, privacy, security, consent, and learner agency. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of learner-owned career decision charter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define a personally meaningful career outcome with observable success, guardrails, tradeoffs, and a review date.
Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily.
Model time, money, energy, care, health, and learning capacity under conservative search scenarios and escalation thresholds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Success Definition
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A candidate with limited runway, caregiving duties, an access need, location limits, and two acceptable work arrangements is pressured to maximize application volume. The workshop variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Success Definition.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define a personally meaningful career outcome with observable success, guardrails, tradeoffs, and a review date. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Success Definition, keep Success Definition connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A candidate with limited runway, caregiving duties, an access need, location limits, and two acceptable work arrangements is pressured to maximize application volume. The workshop variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Success Definition condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: CareerOneStop Job Search — Controls the connected search flow across planning, employer research, networking, applications, portfolios, interviews, background checks, and negotiation.

hints: For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Success Definition, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Success Definition precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Commit to the mission model · Workshop Success Definition, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A candidate with limited runway, caregiving duties, an access need, location limits, and two acceptable work arrangements is pressured to maximize application volume. The workshop variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Success Definition, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: A successful search has one universal outcome: the highest available salary.

options.text: A successful search has one universal outcome: the highest available salary. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Success Definition.

For commit to the mission model, apply Success Definition without its stated preconditions, omit the learner-owned career decision charter trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Success Definition for learner-owned career decision charter.
State the Success Definition preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Agency Review Loop without its stated preconditions, omit the learner-owned career decision charter trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Agency Review Loop for learner-owned career decision charter.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Agency Review Loop
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Agency Review Loop: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A candidate with limited runway, caregiving duties, an access need, location limits, and two acceptable work arrangements is pressured to maximize application volume. The workshop variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Create a learner-controlled review cadence that adapts tactics from evidence without treating rejection as identity evidence.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Agency Review Loop.
steps.why: Retrieving Agency Review Loop through the concrete learner-owned career decision charter case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Agency Review Loop, keep Agency Review Loop connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create a learner-controlled review cadence that adapts tactics from evidence without treating rejection as identity evidence.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Agency Review Loop, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Agency Review Loop precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Agency Review Loop, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A candidate with limited runway, caregiving duties, an access need, location limits, and two acceptable work arrangements is pressured to maximize application volume. The workshop variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Create a learner-controlled review cadence that adapts tactics from evidence without treating rejection as identity evidence.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Agency Review Loop, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Any rejection proves the candidate chose the wrong career.
